title: bursera glabra
intro: Bursera glabra, also known as the Black Myrrh or Gumbo Limbo tree, is a tropical tree native to Central and South America. The resin of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, making it effective against various health issues. The health benefits of Bursera glabra include reducing inflammation and pain, treating skin conditions, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The resin of this tree can be prepared into herbal preparations such as ointments, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
